---
title: "LMEResult"
generated: true
description: "Block-Woodbury (R2) mixed-model fit output -- one correlated / diagonal random slope `(1 + x | g)` / `(x || g)`."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.lme.reml`](../nitrix-stats-lme-reml.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:LMEResult -->
```python
class LMEResult
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:LMEResult -->
Block-Woodbury (R2) mixed-model fit output -- one correlated / diagonal
random slope `(1 + x | g)` / `(x || g)`.

The `lme_fit` dispatcher returns this for the R2 tier; the scalar-intercept
R1 tier returns a `REMLResult` (the FaST-LMM path), and the nested /
crossed / structured-residual tiers their own result types.

Carries the fixed-effect contrast-inference tensors (`fixed_cov` /
`theta_cov` / `grad_m`, audit D3-R2), so `lme_t_contrast` /
`lme_f_contrast` accept it with the **same** Satterthwaite / Kenward-Roger
df machinery as R1.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:LMEResult.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(beta_hat: Float[Array, 'V p'], cov_re: Float[Array, 'V r r'], sigma_e_sq: Float[Array, 'V'], log_lik: Float[Array, 'V'], fixed_cov: Float[Array, 'V p p'], theta_cov: Float[Array, 'V nt nt'], grad_m: Float[Array, 'V nt p p'], tier: str, blups: Optional[Float[Array, 'V q r']] = None) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:LMEResult -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map — voxels, vertices, or fixels |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `r` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `nt` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
